type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Add a module-level function normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] in types.py (near the top, after imports, before ImageBlock class). Behavior: Given raw image bytes that MAY already be base64-encoded, determine whether the input is already base64-encoded. Try base64.b64decode(image, validate=True); if it succeeds without raising, treat input as already-encoded: return (image, decoded_bytes) where decoded_bytes is the b64-decoded raw bytes (for mimetype guessing). If b64decode raises (binascii.Error/ValueError) or otherwise indicates invalid base64, treat input as raw bytes: return (base64.b64encode(image), image) i.e. (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) — encoded_image is always base64-encoded bytes, decoded_for_guessing is always raw (non-base64) bytes suitable for filetype.guess(). Then refactor ImageBlock.image_to_base64 (the @model_validator(mode='after') method) to: if not self.image: return self; else call encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None; then self.image = encoded_image; return self. Need 'import binascii' added if used for exception handling. This preserves existing behavior for raw-bytes input (encodes + guesses mimetype from raw bytes) while adding support for already-base64-encoded input (avoids double-encoding, still guesses mimetype from decoded bytes).
note: normalize_image_bytes + image_to_base64 refactor

type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Add a new method to ImageBlock (llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py, class ImageBlock, near resolve_image): 

def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
    '''Lazily resolve and cache the image mimetype, guessing it only when needed.'''
    if self.image_mimetype is not None:
        return self.image_mimetype
    if self.image:
        guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
    return self.image_mimetype

Behavior: returns self.image_mimetype immediately if already set (cache hit). Otherwise, if self.image bytes are present, runs filetype.guess on them (same logic currently inline in image_to_base64), caches result on self.image_mimetype (may cache None if guess fails), and returns it. If self.image is falsy and no mimetype set, returns None. No signature/behavior for path/url resolution needed (out of scope, matches existing image_to_base64 comment about avoiding resource-intensive path/url loads).

Please refactor the image_to_base64 model_validator to call self.get_image_mimetype() in place of its current inline 'if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(self.image); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None' block, calling it BEFORE the base64.b64encode(self.image) line (so the mimetype guess still runs against raw bytes, not base64-encoded bytes). This just moves the guessing logic into the new public method for reuse; net behavior of image_to_base64 must stay unchanged.
note: Conceding ownership to you since you claimed too - please implement this SPEC alongside your normalize_image_bytes() work. I will not touch types.py.
